topic: AWS Step Functions - serverless workflow orchestration service

sources.title: What Is AWS Step Functions? - Developer Guide
keyInsights: Core architecture: state machines, states, executions, tasks, activities
Standard vs Express workflow comparison
Three service integration patterns: Request-Response, Run a Job, Wait for Callback
220+ AWS service integrations

sources.title: Amazon States Language (ASL) Documentation
keyInsights: JSON-based structured language for state machine definitions
JSONata query language with {% %} syntax
State types: Task, Choice, Fail, Pass, Succeed, Wait, Parallel, Map
Variable assignment via Assign field
.asl.json file extension convention

sources.title: AWS Step Functions Product Page
keyInsights: Visual workflow development with drag-and-drop
220+ service integrations
Agentic AI workflow support
ETL automation, microservices orchestration use cases

sources.title: Standard vs Express Workflows Comparison
keyInsights: Standard: exactly-once, 1 year max, per-transition pricing
Express async: at-least-once; Express sync: at-most-once
Express: 100K/sec start rate, 5-minute max
Workflow type is immutable after creation
Synchronous Express console timeout at 60 seconds

sources.title: Error Handling in Step Functions
keyInsights: Retry fields: ErrorEquals, IntervalSeconds, MaxAttempts, BackoffRate, MaxDelaySeconds, JitterStrategy
Catch fields: ErrorEquals, Next, ResultPath
Predefined errors: States.ALL, States.Timeout, States.TaskFailed, States.Runtime, States.DataLimitExceeded
Processing order: Retry first, then Catch
States.ALL cannot catch States.DataLimitExceeded or States.Runtime

sources.title: Supported Service Integrations
keyInsights: Over 200 AWS SDK integrations
Optimized integrations for Lambda, ECS, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, Bedrock, Glue, etc.
Full pattern support (all 3) for Bedrock, ECS/Fargate, EKS, Step Functions
DynamoDB: Request-Response only

sources.title: Nested Workflows
keyInsights: Start child executions via StartExecution API
AWS_STEP_FUNCTIONS_STARTED_BY_EXECUTION_ID links parent-child
Retry StepFunctions.ExecutionLimitExceeded for throttling
Benefits: modularity, reusability, reduced complexity

sources.title: Distributed Map State
keyInsights: Up to 10,000 concurrent child executions
ItemReader for S3 (CSV, JSON), ItemBatcher for grouping, ResultWriter for S3 export
ToleratedFailurePercentage and ToleratedFailureCount
Child executions: EXPRESS (default) or STANDARD
MaxConcurrency and MaxConcurrencyPath for dynamic control

sources.title: Service Integration Patterns
keyInsights: Request-Response: immediate proceed after HTTP response
.sync: polls and waits for job completion
.waitForTaskToken: pauses until external callback with token
Resource ARN format: arn:aws:states:::service:action[.pattern]
Heartbeat configuration for callback patterns

sources.title: Step Functions Pricing
keyInsights: Standard: $0.000025 per state transition
Express: $1.00/million requests + $0.00001667/GB-second
Free tier: 4,000 transitions/month (never expires)
Memory formula: 50 MB + definition + execution data

sources.title: Workflow Studio
keyInsights: Three modes: Design (drag-and-drop), Code (ASL editor), Config
Auto-sync between Design and Code modes
Patterns tab for pre-built reusable blocks
VS Code integration for individual state testing
Infrastructure Composer integration for SAM/CloudFormation

sources.title: CloudWatch Logs Integration
keyInsights: Four log levels: ALL, ERROR, FATAL, OFF
Express workflows require CloudWatch Logs for execution history
Logs delivered best-effort (not guaranteed complete)
Payloads > 248 KiB truncated in logs
Prefix log groups with /aws/vendedlogs/states/ to avoid policy limits

sources.title: AWS CDK Step Functions Module
keyInsights: DefinitionBody.fromChainable() for programmatic definitions
State classes: Pass, Choice, Parallel, Map, DistributedMap, Wait, Succeed, Fail
Chain.start() and .next() for fluent chaining
JSONata support via *.jsonata() factory methods
addRetry(), addCatch() for error handling
JsonPath helper methods for intrinsic functions

sources.title: Step Functions Best Practices
keyInsights: Nest Express inside Standard for cost optimization
Use S3 for payloads > 256 KiB
Always set TimeoutSeconds on Task/Activity states
Handle Lambda transient errors with specific error codes
Maintain 100+ open polls per activity ARN
Use /aws/vendedlogs/ prefix for log groups

sources.title: Lambda Integration
keyInsights: Three invocation modes: sync, async (InvocationType: Event), callback
Automatic JSON parsing of Lambda Payload
Direct function ARN vs states:::lambda:invoke resource
Heartbeat timeout starts after function completes (sync mode)

sources.title: DynamoDB Integration
keyInsights: Optimized: GetItem, PutItem, UpdateItem, DeleteItem only
No Query or Scan via optimized integration (use AWS SDK)
DynamoDB attribute type descriptors required ({"S": "value"})
Exception prefix: 'DynamoDB' (uppercase) for optimized integration

sources.title: Amazon Bedrock Integration
keyInsights: InvokeModel for inference, CreateModelCustomizationJob for fine-tuning
S3 Input/Output for large payloads exceeding 256 KiB
Supports foundation models, provisioned models, custom models
Prompt engineering via dynamic Body parameters

sources.title: SQS Integration
keyInsights: SendMessage with MessageAttributes support
Callback pattern embeds TaskToken in message body
JSONata expressions for dynamic values
PascalCase parameter convention

sources.title: ECS/Fargate Integration
keyInsights: RunTask action with .sync and .waitForTaskToken patterns
ContainerOverrides for command and environment variables
Task token passed via environment variable for callback
Non-empty Failures field triggers AmazonECS.Unknown error

sources.title: SNS Integration
keyInsights: Publish action with MessageAttributes
Callback pattern with task token
Dynamic value mapping with JSONata

sources.title: EventBridge Integration
keyInsights: PutEvents with automatic execution ARN tracking
Non-zero FailedEntryCount triggers EventBridge.FailedEntry error
Callback pattern support
Dynamic and static resource IAM policies

sources.title: API Gateway Integration
keyInsights: Direct endpoint invocation without AWS SDK
Supports IAM, No Auth, Lambda Authorizers
REST and HTTP APIs (regional only)
Error format: ApiGateway.<HTTP Status Code>
Callback pattern via .waitForTaskToken

sources.title: AWS Glue Integration
keyInsights: StartJobRun with .sync pattern support
JobName extracted from request and added to response
Glue has no resource-based IAM control

sources.title: Activities (External Workers)
keyInsights: Polling model: GetActivityTask, SendTaskSuccess/Failure/Heartbeat
Can wait up to 1 year with heartbeats
Standard workflows only
Maintain 100+ open polls per activity ARN
Not versioned; create new activity for breaking changes

sources.title: Versioning and Aliases
keyInsights: Versions are immutable numbered snapshots
Aliases route traffic to up to 2 versions
Enables blue/green and canary deployments
Revisions are internal snapshots with UUID, no ARN

sources.title: Service Quotas and Limits
keyInsights: 256 KiB payload limit (hard)
1 MB definition size (hard)
25,000 event history limit (Standard)
100,000 state machines per account
1,000,000 open executions per account
10,000 parallel Map children (hard)
Token bucket rate limiting for API calls

sources.title: Monitoring Map Runs
keyInsights: Map Run Details page shows status, ARN, configuration
Item statuses: Pending, Running, Succeeded, Failed, Aborted
Failed items inherit failure from child execution
90-day child execution retention
Recover dropdown for redrive or new execution

sources.title: TestState API
keyInsights: Test individual states without deploying
Three inspection levels: INFO, DEBUG, TRACE
Mock service integrations for unit testing
Validate retry/catch behavior and data transformations
STRICT, PRESENT, NONE validation modes

sources.title: Execution Redrives
keyInsights: Restart from failure point, skip successful steps
Retry counts reset to 0 on redrive
14-day eligibility window
State-specific redrive behavior for each state type
Redriven transitions count as billable transitions

sources.title: Workflow Variables
keyInsights: Assign field for setting variables across steps
256 KiB per variable, 10 MiB per execution
Workflow-local scope; Parallel/Map create child scopes
Distributed Map cannot reference outer scope
Reserved $states variable for input, result, errorOutput, context

sources.title: Intrinsic Functions Reference
keyInsights: 18 intrinsic functions for array, string, math, encoding, hash, UUID operations
JSONPath only (not available with JSONata)
Up to 10 nested intrinsic functions per field
States.Format for string interpolation
States.UUID for unique identifier generation

sources.title: State Transitions
keyInsights: StartAt defines entry state, Next defines transitions
End: true marks terminal states
Choice state allows multiple transition rules
States can have multiple incoming transitions
Redrives count as state transitions for billing

sources.title: CloudWatch Metrics Reference
keyInsights: Execution metrics: Started, Succeeded, Failed, Aborted, TimedOut, Throttled
Express-specific: BilledDuration, BilledMemory
Service integration metrics separate from Lambda metrics
Map Run metrics: OpenMapRunCount, BacklogSize
Recommended: Sum for counts, Average for durations

sources.title: X-Ray Tracing
keyInsights: Enable via console or TracingConfiguration API
Default sampling: 1/sec + 5% additional
Service maps show dependencies and error rates
Not supported for Distributed Map child executions
traceHeader parameter for upstream service integration

sources.title: Step Functions Local
keyInsights: Docker-based local testing (unsupported, limited parity)
AWS recommends TestState API instead
Configurable endpoints for Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS
Integration with SAM CLI for local Lambda
Express workflow logs stored to local files

sources.title: Tutorial: Create Lambda State Machine
keyInsights: Step-by-step: create Lambda, create state machine, test execution
Lambda and state machine must be in same account and region
80-character name limit, ASCII only for CloudWatch compatibility

sources.title: Bedrock Prompt Chaining Sample
keyInsights: Chain multiple prompts sequentially for higher quality output
Cohere Command LLM example
Deploy via CloudFormation template
Optimized Bedrock service integration

sources.title: SAM AWS::Serverless::StateMachine
keyInsights: DefinitionUri or inline Definition property
DefinitionSubstitutions for runtime value injection
AutoPublishAlias for continuous deployments
Events property for schedule and event-driven triggers
Tracing and Logging configuration

sources.title: Sample: Transfer Data with Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS
keyInsights: Iterative DynamoDB read and SQS send pattern
CloudFormation-deployed demo workflow
Lambda populates initial data
For-loop iteration through table entries
